title: Cohort Definitions
description: Cohort Definitions allow researchers to specify inclusion and exclusion criteria using clinical concepts, temporal logic, and demographic filters to identify patient populations of interest. Well-constructed cohorts are the foundation of any reproducible real-world evidence study on the Parthenon platform.
tips: Use concept sets to group related clinical codes and apply them consistently across multiple cohort definitions.
Add index event criteria before applying inclusion rules to improve query performance on large datasets.
Version your cohort definitions and document changes so collaborators can track the evolution of your phenotype.
Validate cohort counts against known prevalence estimates to detect potential misconfiguration early.
Apply washout periods to ensure patients have sufficient observation time prior to their index event.
